title: Harira

overview.description: A hearty Moroccan soup of lamb, chickpeas, lentils and tomatoes, gently spiced and finished with fresh herbs and lemon.

ingredients: 100g dried chickpeas, soaked overnight and cooked
method: Heat the olive oil over a medium-high heat.
Brown the lamb well on all sides.
Add the onion, celery and carrots and cook for 8 minutes until softened.
Stir in the garlic and fresh ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the turmeric, cinnamon, ground ginger, coriander and black pepper.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes, cooked chickpeas, lentils and stock.
Bring to the boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer.
Cook for 45–60 minutes until the lamb is tender and the lentils are cooked.
Stir in most of the coriander and parsley.
Add the lemon juice and season to taste.
Serve garnished with the remaining herbs.

learning.did-you-know: Harira is traditionally eaten across Morocco to break the fast during Ramadan, but it's enjoyed throughout the year as a nourishing family meal.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build flavour by browning lamb, layering spices and balancing richness with fresh herbs and lemon.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A hearty one-pot soup that's ideal for batch cooking and makes excellent use of pantry staples and homemade stock.
chapman-tips: Pressure cook the soaked chickpeas separately before starting the soup, or use leftovers from another recipe.
A pressure cooker can also be used after adding the stock—20–25 minutes at pressure gives beautifully tender lamb.
Don't skip the fresh herbs and lemon; they lift the whole dish.
Blend a small ladleful of the soup and stir it back in if you prefer a slightly thicker consistency.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: The flavours deepen overnight and it's often even better the next day.
